title: तिरुचेन्दूर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री तिरुचेन्दूर स्तोत्रम् ॥

तिरुचेन्दूर्निवासाय षण्मुखाय नमो नमः।
देवसेनापतये तुभ्यं शरवणभवाय ते॥१॥

समुद्रतीरवासाय सूरपद्मनिसूदनाय।
तिरुचेन्दूरनाथाय वेलमुरुगनाय ते॥२॥

षडाननं कुमारं च शक्तिहस्तं कृपानिधिम्।
तिरुचेन्दूरवासं तं मुरुगनं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥३॥

शूरसंहारकं वीरं वल्लीदेवसेनापतिम्।
तिरुचेन्दूरदेवं तं नमामि शरणागतः॥४॥

मयूरवाहनं स्कन्दं वेलाञ्चलनिवासिनम्।
कृपासागरमीशानं तिरुचेन्दूरमाश्रये॥५॥

वेदवेदाङ्गतत्त्वज्ञं ब्रह्मज्ञानप्रदायकम्।
सर्वविद्याप्रदं देवं तिरुचेन्दूरनायकम्॥६॥

तारकासुरसंहर्त्रे क्रौञ्चगिरिविदारिणे।
देवानां रक्षकं शूरं तिरुचेन्दूरवल्लभम्॥७॥

तिरुचेन्दूरस्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः स्कन्दलोकमवाप्नुयात्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री तिरुचेन्दूर स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Thiruchendur Stotram ||

Tiruchendur-nivasaya Shanmukhaya Namo Namah.
Deva-senapataye tubhyam Sharavanabhavaya te.||1||

Samudra-tira-vasaya Sura-padma-nisudanaya.
Tiruchendur-nathaya Vela-muruganaya te.||2||

Shad-ananam Kumaram cha Shakti-hastam Kripaanidhim.
Tiruchendur-vasam tam Muruganam pranamamy-aham.||3||

Shura-samharakam Viram Valli-Devasenaapatim.
Tiruchendur-devam tam Namami sharanaagatah.||4||

Mayura-vahanam Skandam Velanchala-nivasinam.
Kripa-sagaram-Ishanam Tiruchenduram-ashraye.||5||

Veda-vedanga-tattvajnam Brahma-jnana-pradayakam.
Sarva-vidya-pradam devam Tiruchendur-nayakam.||6||

Tarakasura-samhartre Krauncha-giri-vidarine.
Devanam rakshakam Shuram Tiruchendur-vallabham.||7||

Tiruchendur-stotram yah pathed bhakti-samyutah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmuktah Skanda-lokam avapnuyat.||8||

|| Iti Shri Thiruchendur Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: तिरुचेन्दूर निवासी, षण्मुख, देवसेनापति, शरवणभव को नमस्कार। समुद्रतट पर निवास करने वाले, सूरपद्म का संहार करने वाले, तिरुचेन्दूर के स्वामी वेलमुरुगन को नमस्कार। षडानन, कुमार, शक्तिहस्त, कृपानिधि, तिरुचेन्दूरवासी मुरुगन को प्रणाम करता हूँ। शूरसंहारक वीर, वल्ली-देवसेनापति, तिरुचेन्दूर देव को शरणागत होकर नमस्कार करता हूँ। इस स्तोत्र के भक्तिपूर्वक पाठ से सर्वपापमुक्ति और स्कन्दलोक की प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Praises Lord Murugan at his Thiruchendur abode
Specially connected to the seaside temple of Kartikeya
Frees from all sins and leads to Skanda-loka
Invokes knowledge of vedas and brahman
Powerful for seekers of divine protection and wisdom
description: Thiruchendur Stotram (तिरुचेन्दूर स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Kartikeya. This revered text originates from South Indian Tradition. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to specially connected to the seaside temple of Kartikeya as well as grant knowledge of Vedas and Brahman. Additionally, it is known to praise Lord Murugan at his Thiruchendur abode. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Skanda Shashti to invoke divine grace. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Thiruchendur Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Thiruchendur Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Lord Murugan at Thiruchendur for protection, wisdom and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Thiruchendur Stotram and which temple does it celebrate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Thiruchendur Stotram is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn dedicated to Lord Murugan (Kartikeya, Skanda, Subrahmanya, Shanmukha) at his celebrated Thiruchendur temple — the Sri Subrahmanya Swamy Temple at Thiruchendur, Tuticorin district, Tamil Nadu, on the coast of the Gulf of Mannar. Thiruchendur is the second of the Arupadai Veedu, the six sacred abodes of Murugan in Tamil Nadu (the others being Palani, Swamimalai, Pazhamudircholai, Thiruparankundram and Pazhani). It is the only one of the six built directly on the seashore and is uniquely associated with Murugan's victory over the asura Surapadma. The stotram opens with Thiruchendur-nivasaya Shanmukhaya Namo Namah — salutations to the six-faced Lord who dwells at Thiruchendur. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Tiruchendur Sthala Purana; Tamil Nadu HRCE temple records.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the historical significance of Thiruchendur in the Surapadma legend?
seoFaqs.answer: Thiruchendur is the site where Lord Murugan, after a six-day battle (commemorated as the Skanda Shashti or Soorasamharam festival), slew the asura Surapadma. The stotram explicitly invokes this in verse 2 with Sura-padma-nisudanaya — the destroyer of Surapadma. According to the Skanda Purana, Surapadma had taken the form of a giant mango tree in the ocean. Murugan split the tree with his Vel (divine spear), and the two halves became the peacock (his vahana) and the rooster (his banner — kukkuta-dhvaja). The site of this battle became Thiruchendur, where the temple was built facing the sea where Surapadma fell. The annual Soorasamharam festival re-enacts this battle on the seashore in October-November. (Sources: Skanda Purana Bala Khanda; Thiruchendur Sthala Purana; Tamil Nadu HRCE.)

seoFaqs.question: What aspects of Murugan does the Thiruchendur Stotram highlight?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram invokes Murugan under his classical names and attributes. Verse 1: Shanmukha (six-faced), Devasenapati (commander of the deva army), Sharavanabhava (born in the reed forest). Verse 2: Samudra-tira-vasa (resident of the seashore), Vela-murugan (Murugan of the Vel spear). Verse 3: Shadanana, Kumara, Shakti-hasta (holding the divine shakti spear), Kripaanidhi (treasure of grace). Verse 4: Shura-samharaka (slayer of Sura), Vira (hero), Valli-Devasena-pati (consort of Valli and Devasena). Verse 5: Mayura-vahana (peacock-mounted), Velanchala-nivasin (resident of the seashore mountain). Verse 6: Veda-vedanga-tattvajna (knower of Vedas and their auxiliaries), Brahma-jnana-pradayaka (giver of Brahman-knowledge). Verse 7: Tarakasura-samharta and Krauncha-giri-vidarin (splitter of Mount Krauncha). (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Thiruchendur Stotram verse-by-verse.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Thiruchendur Stotram be chanted for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalvar) is the day prescribed for Kartikeya in North Indian tradition (sharing the day with his role as devasenapati and warrior, and with Mangala the planet Mars), while Friday (Velli kizhamai) and Krittika nakshatra days are emphasised in Tamil Nadu. The stotram is especially chanted during Skanda Shashti (the six-day fasting and puja festival in Aippasi/Karthikai October-November culminating in the grand Soorasamharam at Thiruchendur), during Thai Pusam (Pushya nakshatra in Tamil month Thai, Jan-Feb), on Vaikasi Visakam (Vishakha nakshatra in Vaisakha celebrating Murugan's appearance), Krittika nakshatra of every month (Krittikai), and during pilgrimage to Thiruchendur or any of the Arupadai Veedu. (Sources: Drikpanchang Skanda Shashti; Thiruchendur temple festival calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Thiruchendur Stotram grant to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti (verse 8) declares Thiruchendur-stotram yah pathed bhakti-samyutah, sarva-papa-vinirmuktah Skanda-lokam avapnuyat — the devotee who recites this stotram with devotion becomes free of all sins and reaches Skanda-loka, the celestial abode of Kartikeya. The stotram explicitly invokes Murugan as Brahma-jnana-pradayaka (giver of Brahman-knowledge) and Sarva-vidya-prada (giver of all knowledge), making it especially powerful for spiritual seekers and students. As Shura-samharaka and Devasenapati, the stotram grants victory over enemies, courage in adversity, removal of Mangala-dosha and Kuja-dosha (since Kartikeya is identified with Mars), success for warriors, soldiers, athletes, and protection during sea travel (since Thiruchendur is the seaside abode). (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; Tamil Murugan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Thiruchendur Stotram relate to other Murugan stotras and the Arupadai Veedu?
seoFaqs.answer: Thiruchendur is the second of the Arupadai Veedu — the six sacred abodes of Murugan: Thiruparankundram (the wedding abode), Thiruchendur (the seashore battle abode), Palani (the renunciate abode where Murugan stands as a sannyasi), Swamimalai (the teacher abode where Murugan taught Pranava to Shiva), Thiruthani (the peace abode after marrying Valli), and Pazhamudircholai (the forest abode where he is with Valli and Devasena). Each abode has its own stotra and sthala-purana. The Thiruchendur Stotram is paired most often with Subrahmanya Bhujangam by Adi Shankaracharya (composed at this very temple, 33 verses in Bhujanga-prayata metre), Skanda Shashti Kavacham (244-line Tamil composition by Devaraya Swamigal), and the Shanmukha Stotram. (Sources: Tamil Murugan tradition; Stotra Nidhi Skanda collection.)
